column1: Of course, your feelings of success are short-lived, since this is where Ken Williams has to tell you about our soon-to-be-released sequel.\n\nHey, Ken! Come here, and give 'em your pitch...
column2: 當然，你的勝利快感短暫得很，因為這裡非得讓肯威廉斯跟你聊聊我們即將推出的續作。\n\n喂，肯！過來，給他們推銷一下…

column1: Hi, folks! Remember, coming to this very computer Real Soon Now, is the next installment in the saga of Larry Laffer, traveling software salesman. I'd tell you the name now, but we haven't made one up yet!\n\n(Be sure to pick up a few extra copies, just in case our disk copier is screwed up again!)
column2: 嗨，各位！記住，很快就要在這部電腦上推出 Larry Laffer 旅行推銷員傳奇的下一章了。我很想現在就告訴你片名，可我們還沒想好呢！\n\n(最好多買幾份，免得我們的軟碟複製機又出問題!)

column1: Oh, wait. We almost forget to tell you how well you played.
column2: 噢，等等。我們差點忘了評評你玩得怎樣。

column1: You can't bear the thought of deflating your new friend, so you don't!
column2: 你捨不得戳破你新交的朋友，所以作罷。

column1: What fun is an uninflated inflatable doll?
column2: 充氣娃娃沒氣，還有什麼意思？

column1: ...thanks for playing, and be sure to tell each and every one of your friends to buy their own personal copy of this game.
column2: …感謝遊玩，別忘了跟你每一個朋友吹捧這款遊戲，叫他們去買。

column1: On behalf of Mark and Sandy, Al and Marg, Jeff and Chris, Russ and Kim, Larry and Bob, Ken and Roberta, Bob and Marilyn and/or Bob and Jeannine, John and Mary, Bob and Carol and Ted and Alice...
column2: 代表 Mark 和 Sandy、Al 和 Marg、Jeff 和 Chris、Russ 和 Kim、Larry 和 Bob、Ken 和 Roberta、Bob 和 Marilyn 或/和 Bob 和 Jeannine、John 和 Mary、Bob 和 Carol 和 Ted 和 Alice…

column1: Don't you find it difficult to have a meaningful relationship with latex?
column2: 你不覺得跟橡膠娃娃建立有意義的關係很難嗎？
抱歉。只有一個是你的極限！

column1: You don't have time to rest!
Perhaps if you first established mouth/latex contact...
column2: 說不定先來個嘴對橡膠的接觸…

column1: Picking up the corner of the expensive, vinyl, zebra-skin bedspread, you discover...
column2: 你掀起那張昂貴的乙烯基斑馬紋床罩一角，發現…

column1: That's not what she's for, Larry.
...and that's exactly the way she used to look!

column2: 女士們、先生們…\n\n肯威廉斯先生！
